figures.caption: Table 6: Distributions of all variables used to cluster SENECA data by BFMM[VVV].

Figure 5: Distributions of the six clinical endpoints by BFMM[VVV] clustering results of SENECA dataset. The annotated p-values are based on Chi-squared test comparing the proportions among the four clusters.

Figure 7: Distributions of the three clinical endpoints by BFMM[EEI] clustering results of EDEN trial dataset. The annotated p-values are based on Chi-squared test comparing the proportions among the three clusters in the top panel, while between the two feeding groups in each cluster in the bottom panel.
Figure 1: Illustrative bivariate 3-cluster mixture density plots for EEI, EEE, and VVV structures.
Table 1: Parameterizations of the covariance structures accommodated in BFMMs
Table 7: EDEN Trial averaged variable importance weights by BFMM[EEI] given 3 clusters.
Table 8: Distributions of identified important variables by BFMM[EEI] for EDEN trial data.
Table 3: Simulated three-cluster mixed data categorical variable generation parameters.
Table 2: Simulated three-cluster mixed data continuous variable generation parameters.

Figure 4: BIC and ICL evaluation for SENECA data by BFMM given G = 1, . . . , 9 clusters. Information criteria evaluation used Markov chains each taking 10000 Gibbs sampling iterations with 5000 for burn-in.

Figure 6: BIC and ICL evaluation for EDEN trial data by BFMM given G = 1, . . . , 6 clusters. Information criteria evaluation used Markov chains each taking 5000 Gibbs sampling iterations with 2500 for burn-in.
Table 4: A comparison between the 4 clusters identified by BFMM[VVV] and by mclust[VVV].
Table 5: SENECA averaged variable importance weights by BFMM[VVV] given 4 clusters.

Figure 2: Graphical representation of the proposed Bayesian FMM framework given VVV covariance structure - BFMM[VVV]. Subscripts i, g,m denote the ith observation (i = 1, . . . , n), gth cluster (g = 1, . . . , G), and mth variable (m = 1, . . . , q if continuous, m = q + 1, . . . ,M if categorical), respectively.

Figure 3: Violin plots summarizing adjusted rand index (ARI) by different clustering methods in each simulated scenario. Categorical variables in simulated datasets were treated as continuous when applying the mclust and bootstrap K-means clustering methods.
